reasoning: The informal statement explicitly says "X is a nonempty complete metric space." The original formalization omits the `[Nonempty X]` typeclass, while the corrected version includes it. Without nonemptiness, X could be the empty type (which has a valid MetricSpace and CompleteSpace instance in Mathlib, confirmed in Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Defs.lean). For an empty X, every set is vacuously dense and open, but no `x` exists to satisfy `∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ G n`. Hence the original statement has a concrete counterexample and is provably false. The original is strictly stronger than the informal claim (it implies the informal but asserts more by covering empty spaces as well). The error is dropping the explicitly stated "nonempty" hypothesis from the informal problem, hence missing_premise.
